Description: Text is typed entirely in capital letters. TTS engines may read each letter individually or stress words unnaturally.
Remediation: Use mixed-case text. Apply a character style or CSS rule with text-transform:uppercase rather than typing in capitals.

Description: Alt text appears to be a filename rather than a meaningful description.
Remediation: Replace the filename with a description of what the image shows and why it matters in context.

Description: Alt text begins with 'image of', 'picture of', or 'photo of'. Screen readers already announce the element is an image.
Remediation: Remove the redundant prefix and begin the alt text with the actual description.

Description: Alt text is present but very short (fewer than 3 characters) and unlikely to convey meaningful information.
Remediation: Expand the alt text to describe the image content and its purpose in context.

Description: A raw URL appears in text without descriptive link text. Screen readers read the URL character by character.
Remediation: Wrap the URL in a Markdown link: [Descriptive text](https://url).

Description: An Excel Table column header is blank or uses a generic label like Column1.
Remediation: Click the header cell and enter a concise, descriptive label.

MSOfficeCategory: Use of blank cells for formatting
Description: Multiple consecutive blank rows within data suggest blank rows are used for visual spacing.
Remediation: Remove blank rows used for spacing. Use row height and cell padding via Format Cells instead.

Description: A Pandoc pipe table has one or more blank column header cells.
Remediation: Add a descriptive label to each column header cell.

Description: A chart has no alt text. Without alt text a blind user receives no information about the chart's content or key finding.
Remediation: Select the chart, right-click its border, choose Edit Alt Text, and describe the chart's key finding.

Description: A chart axis has no title label. Users cannot determine what quantity or categories are represented.
Remediation: Select the chart and add axis titles via Chart Design > Add Chart Element > Axis Titles. Include units where applicable.

MSOfficeCategory: Object has no title or name
Description: A chart has no title. Without a title, users cannot identify what the chart represents.
Remediation: Select the chart, go to Chart Design > Add Chart Element > Chart Title, and enter a descriptive title.

Description: A fenced code block has no language specifier. Screen reader users receive no cue about what kind of code they are reading.
Remediation: Add a language identifier after the opening fence: ```python, ```bash, ```json, etc.

Description: Empty cells with background fill color may be conveying status through color alone.
Remediation: Add a text label or symbol inside the colored cell that conveys the same meaning as the color.

Description: Table has header cells in both the first row and first column. Screen readers may not correctly associate data cells with both row and column headers.
Remediation: Verify screen reader navigation. If possible, restructure into simpler tables. Test with NVDA or JAWS.

MSOfficeCategory: Object has no title or name
Description: A worksheet tab has a default name (Sheet1, Sheet2, etc.). Screen readers announce the tab name when navigating.
Remediation: Right-click the tab and choose Rename. Enter a concise, descriptive name.

MSOfficeCategory: Object has no title or name
Description: An Excel Table has a default name (Table1, Table2, etc.).
Remediation: Click inside the table, go to Table Design, and replace the default name with a descriptive identifier. No spaces allowed.

Description: Two headings at the same level share identical text, creating ambiguous navigation in screen reader heading lists.
Remediation: Make each heading uniquely descriptive of its section.

Description: A heading marker is present but contains no text. Empty headings create phantom navigation stops.
Remediation: Add descriptive text after the heading marker, or remove the marker if no heading is intended.

Description: A Markdown link has no visible text. Screen readers read the raw URL or produce no output.
Remediation: Add descriptive text between the square brackets that describes where the link leads.

Description: A table cell is empty. Screen reader users may be uncertain whether the cell is intentionally blank.
Remediation: Add content, use a dash or N/A for missing data, or restructure the table to eliminate unnecessary cells.

Description: An entire line is bold. Bolding long passages reduces the emphasis signal for screen reader users.
Remediation: Use bold sparingly for key terms. If the intent is a heading, use a heading marker instead.

Description: Three or more consecutive blank lines used for visual spacing.
Remediation: Remove extra blank lines. Use a single blank line to separate paragraphs.

Description: A line has more than two trailing spaces. Exactly two trailing spaces are an intentional hard line break in Pandoc Markdown; more than two is almost always unintentional.
Remediation: Remove the extra trailing spaces.

Description: A Unicode bullet character is used as an inline list marker rather than a Markdown list item.
Remediation: Use proper Markdown list items: start each item on its own line with - or * followed by a space.

Description: A paragraph uses a manually typed bullet character instead of the application's built-in list style.
Remediation: Apply the List Bullet built-in style (Word), a bullet list from the Home tab (PowerPoint), or - / * syntax (Markdown).

Description: Lines appear to be a manually numbered list outside proper Markdown ordered list syntax.
Remediation: Use Markdown ordered list syntax: start each item with '1.' followed by a space.

Description: A slide auto-advances in less than 3 seconds, which may not give screen reader users enough time to hear the content.
Remediation: Increase the auto-advance time to at least 5 seconds, or disable auto-advance. Go to Transitions > Advance Slide > After.

Description: A slide uses Fast transition speed. Rapid visual motion can trigger symptoms in users with vestibular disorders.
Remediation: Set the transition speed to Medium or Slow in the Transitions tab Duration field.

Description: A floating text box is read by screen readers in insertion order, which may differ from visual reading order.
Remediation: Convert to inline body text if possible. Otherwise verify reading order in the Selection Pane.

Description: A large worksheet has no frozen panes. The header row scrolls out of view, removing column context for screen reader users.
Remediation: Click cell A2, go to View > Freeze Panes > Freeze Panes.

MSOfficeCategory: Object has no title or name
Description: A heading ends with a period, semicolon, or colon, causing TTS to insert an unnatural pause.
Remediation: Remove the trailing punctuation from the heading.

MSOfficeCategory: Object has no title or name
Description: A heading is over 120 characters, which is difficult to scan in a screen reader heading list.
Remediation: Shorten the heading to under 80 characters. Move additional content into the paragraph below.

Description: Hidden columns disrupt the continuous data sequence and may be skipped by screen readers.
Remediation: Unhide columns. Consider moving the data to a separate sheet or documenting hidden columns with a visible note.

Description: Hidden rows disrupt the continuous data sequence and may be skipped by screen readers.
Remediation: Unhide rows. Consider using grouping (Data > Group) or filters instead.

Description: A hyperlink is not underlined, making it indistinguishable from body text for users with color vision deficiencies.
Remediation: Ensure the Hyperlink character style includes underlining: Home > Styles > right-click Hyperlink > Modify.

Description: An indented code block (4-space indent) cannot carry a language specifier.
Remediation: Convert to a fenced code block using ``` with a language identifier.

Description: More than 20 paragraphs appear without any heading. Screen reader users rely on headings to navigate.
Remediation: Add appropriately leveled headings to mark major sections.

Description: Merged cells disrupt screen reader table navigation and break sort and filter operations.
Remediation: Remove merged cells: Home > Merge & Center > Unmerge Cells. Use Center Across Selection for visual centering.

Description: An image, shape, chart, or other non-text object has no alternative text.
Remediation: Right-click the object, choose Edit Alt Text, and write a concise description. If purely decorative, mark as decorative.

Description: The document Author property is not set.
Remediation: Go to File > Info > Properties and enter the author name. In Markdown, add author: to the YAML front matter.

Description: No description field is present in the Pandoc YAML front matter.
Remediation: Add description: with a one-to-two sentence summary to the YAML front matter.

Description: The document's proofing language is not set. Screen readers use this to select the correct TTS voice.
Remediation: Go to Review > Language > Set Proofing Language and select the appropriate language.

Description: The document Title property is not set. Screen readers announce the title when the document is opened.
Remediation: Go to File > Info > Properties and enter a descriptive title in the Title field.

Description: No lang field in the YAML front matter. Pandoc uses this to set the HTML lang attribute and PDF language metadata.
Remediation: Add lang: en-US (or appropriate BCP 47 tag) to the YAML front matter.

Description: The presentation Author property is not set.
Remediation: Go to File > Info > Properties and enter the author name.

Description: The presentation Title property is not set. Screen readers announce the title when the file is opened.
Remediation: Go to File > Info > Properties and enter a descriptive title.

Description: No title field in the YAML front matter. Screen readers announce the document title when it is opened.
Remediation: Add title: "My Document Title" to the YAML front matter.

Description: The workbook Author property is not set.
Remediation: Go to File > Info > Properties and enter the author name.

Description: The workbook Title property is not set. Screen readers announce the title when the file is opened.
Remediation: Go to File > Info > Properties and enter a descriptive title.

Description: A raw HTML marquee or blink element causes moving or blinking content, violating WCAG 2.2.2.
Remediation: Remove the element entirely. Moving and blinking text has no accessible equivalent.

Description: A document of substantial length has no headings. Screen reader users navigate long documents by heading list.
Remediation: Add ATX-style headings (# H1, ## H2) to mark major sections.

Description: No slides have speaker notes. For distributed presentations, notes provide essential context for screen reader users.
Remediation: Add speaker notes via View > Notes. Describe visual content and anything conveyed only through visuals.

Description: The Markdown file has no YAML front matter block. Without front matter, Pandoc cannot set document title, language, or author.
Remediation: Add a YAML front matter block starting and ending with --- at the very top of the file. Include at minimum: title and lang.

Description: Hyperlink display text is non-descriptive or a raw URL. Screen reader users navigating by link list cannot determine the destination.
Remediation: Edit the hyperlink display text to describe the destination. The text should make sense when read in isolation.

Description: Markdown link text is non-descriptive (click here, here, read more, etc.).
Remediation: Replace with text that describes the link destination.

Description: Multiple animations trigger automatically with very short delays, which can overwhelm screen reader users.
Remediation: Increase delays to at least 1 second per element, or switch to On Click triggers.

Description: A raw HTML br tag is used for a line break, reducing portability to non-HTML Pandoc output formats.
Remediation: End the line with two trailing spaces or a backslash for a portable hard line break.

Description: A raw HTML div or span is silently ignored by Pandoc when converting to non-HTML formats.
Remediation: Use Pandoc's native fenced div (::: {.class}) or bracketed span ([text]{.class}) syntax instead.

Description: A raw HTML presentational element (font, center) conveys no semantics and is not supported in non-HTML Pandoc output formats.
Remediation: Replace with Markdown formatting or a Pandoc-native span/div.

Description: A raw HTML table is used. HTML tables require explicit th scope headers and a caption to be accessible.
Remediation: Use Pandoc pipe table syntax, or ensure the HTML table includes th scope headers and a caption.

Description: A paragraph contains consecutive spaces likely used for visual layout.
Remediation: Remove repeated spaces. Use paragraph indentation or tab stops for layout instead.

Description: A repeating or looping animation violates WCAG 2.2.2 because the user cannot stop it without leaving the slide.
Remediation: Set the animation repeat count to 1 in the Effect Options dialog.

MSOfficeCategory: Object has no title or name
Description: A worksheet tab name exceeds 31 characters, which is Excel's maximum.
Remediation: Right-click the tab, choose Rename, and shorten the name to 31 characters or fewer.

Description: The document skips one or more heading levels (e.g., H1 followed directly by H3).
Remediation: Change the heading style to restore sequential order. Every level must appear before a deeper level is used.

MSOfficeCategory: Object has no title or name
Description: A slide has no title or the title placeholder is empty. Screen readers announce slide titles as navigation landmarks.
Remediation: Click the title placeholder and enter a concise, descriptive title.

Description: Text on a slide is smaller than 18pt, which may be too small for users with low vision.
Remediation: Increase font size to at least 18pt for body text and 24pt or larger for key content.

Description: A Pandoc pipe table row has a different number of cells than the header row.
Remediation: Ensure every row has the same number of pipe-delimited cells as the header row.

Description: A Word table does not have a designated header row.
Remediation: Click in the first row, go to Table Design > Table Style Options and check Header Row.

Description: An Excel Table has its header row turned off.
Remediation: Click inside the table, go to Table Design, and check the Header Row checkbox.

Description: The title placeholder is not at the back of the z-order. PowerPoint screen readers traverse shapes from back to front.
Remediation: Open the Selection Pane (Home > Arrange > Selection Pane) and drag the title placeholder to the bottom of the list.

MSOfficeCategory: Object has no title or name
Description: A Word form field has no help text. Screen readers announce only the field type with no indication of what is requested.
Remediation: Open the field's properties and enter a descriptive label in the Status Bar Help Text or Help Key field.

Description: A Markdown link uses a raw URL as its display text. Screen readers read the URL character by character.
Remediation: Replace the URL display text with a description of the link destination.

Description: A slide has images or charts without alt text and no speaker notes, making it entirely inaccessible.
Remediation: Add alt text to each image and chart, and add speaker notes describing the key visual content.
